title: Help with paying
description: If you can't pay on time, there are support options you can access to meet your tax and super obligations.
keywords: paying, payment help, payment plan, payment plans, managing payments, HELP deferral, SFSS deferral, financial difficulty, serious hardship, help with paying, pay tax, bill, debt, hardship, interest, interest-free payments, late payment, make a payment, payment arrangement, payments, tax debts, tax obligations, interest-free, secured payment plan, securities

content_blocks.text: What deferring means
If you have a loan with the ATO (which includes a compulsory repayment or overseas levy) and you can't pay on time, you may apply to defer or amend your compulsory repayment or overseas levy.
This means we will either:

items: reduce the amount of repayment required toward your loan (amend).

content_blocks.text: When to apply to defer
You may apply to defer or amend your compulsory repayment or overseas levy if:

items: making your repayment would cause you serious hardship
there are other special reasons, for example natural disasters affecting you death or serious illness other serious or difficult circumstances.

content_blocks.text: If you apply to defer or amend your repayment under serious hardship, we will ask you for a detailed statement of your household income and expenditure to justify your claim.
We may also ask you to provide further details of income and expenditure and evidence, such as a copy of your most recent payslip.
If you have special circumstances and need to apply to defer or amend your repayment, complete a Defer or amend your compulsory repayment or overseas levy form (NAT 2471).
For more information see Tax support for individuals .
How to apply for deferral
To apply for a deferral see Defer or amend your compulsory repayment or overseas levy .
For more information see Contact us – Individuals enquiries
Support for people experiencing vulnerability

If you're facing challenging personal circumstances and need extra help managing your tax obligations, visit Tax support when you need it most for other support options available to people experiencing vulnerability.
How to use this document
This information is written in a way that is easy to read.
We use pictures to explain some ideas.
We have also provided a PDF of this document so you can read this information offline – If you can't lodge or pay on time - Easy Read (PDF, 3.6MB) This link will download a file

rows: This document has been written by the Australian Tax Office or ATO.
When you see the words 'we', 'our' or 'us' it means the ATO.
This Easy Read document is a summary of another document.
You can find the longer document on our website at www.ato.gov.au/Cant-Lodge-Or-Pay

content_blocks.text: Lodging and paying tax

rows: When you earn money you may need to tell us about it .
This means you need to: lodge a tax return pay your tax bills by the due date pay your study or training loan.
If you do not pay your tax bill by the due date it becomes a tax debt .
A tax debt is when you owe us money. For example, if you did not pay enough tax during the year you will owe us money.
A tax return is a form you fill in each year and give to us. In the form you tell us how much money you made and how much tax you have already paid.

content_blocks.text: If you can not lodge or pay

rows: Talk to us as soon as possible if you can not: lodge a tax return pay your tax debt pay your study or training loan.
To tell us about your situation, phone us on 13 11 42 during operating hours .
We will need to ask you some questions to prove who you are . This is to protect your information.
This document tells you how to:
get help to lodge your tax
set up a payment plan to pay your tax
ask us to change your tax due date
ask us to change your tax amount.

content_blocks.text: Help to lodge

rows: You must lodge your tax return by 31 October.
If you want to understand more about lodging tax we have 2 easier to read documents to help you: Get ready to do your tax How to do your tax
There are many ways to get help with lodging your tax return. You can use:
a tax agent
Tax Help
the National Tax Clinic program.
If you can't use these services, you can phone us on 13 11 42 to talk to us about your situation.
We will need to ask you some questions to prove who you are . This is to protect your information.

content_blocks.text: 1. Tax agent

rows: A tax agent is a person who you pay to do your tax.
Only registered tax agents can ask for money to do your tax.
Registered means they:
have studied to become a tax agent
are on our list of tax agents.
You can search for a registered tax agent at www.tpb.gov.au External Link

content_blocks.text: 2. Tax Help

rows: Tax Help is a group of people who help other people do their tax.
They do not ask for any money to do this.
We have trained these people to know about tax.
Not everyone can use this service.
Find out more about the Tax Help program .

content_blocks.text: 3. National Tax Clinic program

rows: The National Tax Clinic program can help people who: can not pay a tax agent do not meet the rules to use Tax Help are having money or personal problems.
This program is: paid for by the government run by universities and TAFEs.
Students who are learning about tax can help you for free.
Trained managers make sure the students give you the right help.
The students can help you: in person over the phone through a meeting online.
You can also use this program if you are a small business.
You need to meet the rules to use this program.
Find out more about the National Tax Clinic program External Link .

content_blocks.text: Help to pay

rows: We can help you if you can not:
pay all your tax
pay on time.
If you have a tax debt you may be able to set up a payment plan . This is a way to pay using smaller payments over time.
If you have a study or training loan you can ask us to defer or amend your payment . Defer means we can change the date of when you need to pay. Amend means we can change the amount you need to pay.

content_blocks.text: Payment plan
Work out if a payment plan will help you

rows: To work out if a payment plan is a good idea for you, you need to know:
your tax debt
how much you need to pay straight away
how much you can afford to pay each week, fortnight or month. Fortnight means 2 weeks.
You can use our payment plan estimator. The payment plan estimator is an online tool. It can help you work out your payment plan.

content_blocks.text: Use the payment plan estimator

rows: Open the payment plan estimator .
Put in how much your tax debt is. Only put numbers in. Do not put spaces or other symbols in.
Pick when you will pay:
one payment a week one payment a fortnight one payment a month.
a lump sum payment. Lump sum means you pay your whole tax debt at once.
Choose a date. Select 'Show Result' to get your answer.
If you select weekly, fortnightly or monthly payments you will need to pay an upfront amount. Upfront means the amount you must pay within 7 days.
You can change the upfront amount to be more or less money.
Then choose how to work out your payment plan. There are 2 ways you can do this.
You can choose Repayment amounts. You will need to enter how much you will pay each time.
Or you can choose Repayment periods. You will need to enter the start and end dates for your payments.
Select 'Show Result' to get your answer.
The tool will show you how much you must pay: in total upfront for each period in interest.
You can print the results page to keep for your records.

content_blocks.text: Set up a payment plan

rows: After you use the payment plan estimator you can set up your payment plan with us.
Read the important rules about payment plans first.
To set up a payment plan you can:
Use ATO online services.
Use our automated phone service on 13 28 65. Automated means you will not speak to a person. You will select numbers and symbols using your phone.
Phone us on 13 11 42 and speak to a person. We will need to ask you some questions to prove who you are . This is to protect your information.

content_blocks.text: Use ATO online services

rows: You will need a myGov account linked to the ATO .
When you are logged into ATO online services, from the menu choose: Tax then choose Payment then choose Payment plans.
You can:
set up your payment plan
choose to be sent SMS or email reminders about payments
view a summary of your payment plan
change or cancel your payment plan.

content_blocks.text: Use the automated phone service

rows: Call the phone service on 13 28 65 .
You can ask someone you trust to be with you to help you.
Before you call you will need to know your:
tax file number ( TFN ) or Australian business number ( ABN )
total amount of your debt.
Your TFN is a 9-digit number that identifies you in the tax and system.
An ABN is an 11-digit number that a business uses to identify themselves when dealing with other business or with government.
The phone service will tell you how to set up the payment plan. It is best to phone in business hours (8:00 am to 6:00 pm on weekdays Monday to Friday).
We will connect you to our call centre if you can not use the automated phone service.

content_blocks.text: Defer or amend your payment

rows: You may have a compulsory repayment for your study or training loan.
Compulsory repayment means a payment you must make.
You can ask us to defer or amend your compulsory repayment. Defer means you will not have to make a compulsory repayment for the next year.
Amend means your payment for the year will be smaller.
We can defer or amend if paying will be a serious hardship . Serious hardship is when it is hard to pay for basic living costs like food, housing, transport and medical treatment.
You can also ask us to defer or amend your compulsory repayment if you have been affected by:
a natural disaster (like a flood, bushfire or cyclone)
serious illness (like cancer)
the death of a family member or close friend.
You will need to provide us with evidence about your situation .

content_blocks.text: Compulsory repayments you can ask us to defer or amend

rows: You can ask to defer or amend these types of compulsory repayments:

Higher Education Loan Program (HELP) Higher Education Contribution Scheme (HECS) VET Student Loan (VSL) Student Financial Supplement Scheme (SFSS) Student Start-up Loan (SSL) ABSTUDY Student Start-up Loan (ABSTUDY SSL) Australian Apprenticeship Support Loan (AASL) – previously known as Trade Support Loan (TSL) HELP, VSL or AASL overseas levy.

content_blocks.text: How to apply

rows: You need to fill out a form. You can find it at Defer or amend a repayment .
The form has information to help you answer the questions.
You can ask someone you trust to help you fill out the form.
If you can not fill out the form you can phone us on 1300 650 225 and we will help you.
We will need to ask you some questions to prove who you are . This is to protect your information.

content_blocks.text: Need help?

rows: If you need help you can phone us on 13 11 42 .
We will need to ask you some questions to prove who you are . This is to protect your information.
If you have difficulty hearing or speaking to people who use a phone, you can contact us through the National Relay Service (NRS).
You need to: Call the National Relay Service on 13 36 77 ask the Relay Officer to phone us on 13 11 42 .

If you want to speak in a language other than English, phone the Translating and Interpreting Service (TIS National) on 13 14 50 . Tell them: you want to speak to us the language you want to speak in.

content_blocks.text: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander languages
Multicultural languages

items: 简体中文主页/ Simplified Chinese
繁體中文主頁 / Traditional Chinese
ကညီကျိၥ် / Karen
ភាសាខ្មែរ / Khmer

content_blocks.text: Educational support

We offer free educational support to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities and culturally and linguistically diverse communities. We deliver informative, tailored presentations on tax and superannuation in Australia to help people understand their responsibilities, obligations and rights. We can also attend large community events that promote government services, if certain criteria are met.

If you want to know more about the support and education we provide, you can email us at CommunityEducation@ato.gov.au to request a presentation for community groups. All presentations are run virtually.
Watch and listen

items: atoTV – Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander languages External Link
Tax and super experiences – multicultural voices

content_blocks.text: Read

items: Glossary of common tax and superannuation terms
Tax time resources in languages other than English
Forms, publications and factsheets for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples External Link
Forms, publications and factsheets for multicultural communities External Link (search for your language)

content_blocks.text: Contact us
Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples
Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples can phone our Indigenous Helpline on 13 10 30 (8:00 am to 6:00 pm, Monday to Friday, except public holidays).
Our Indigenous Helpline staff specialise in supporting Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples with things like getting a tax file number, lodging a tax return, or finding your super.
Translating and interpreting service
If you would like to speak to us in a language other than English, you can use the Translating and Interpreting Service (TIS National).
This free service provides an interpreter in one of these available languages External Link . The service will connect you and an interpreter to us so we can help with your query.
To contact TIS National, between 8:00 am and 6:00 pm AEST, Monday to Friday, phone:

TIS National does not provide interpreting services for Indigenous languages. Information about Indigenous interpreters External Link is available on the National Indigenous Australians Agency website.
Call services for hearing or speech/communication difficulties
If you are d/Deaf, hard of hearing or have a speech/communication difficulty you can contact us through the National Relay Service External Link (NRS), as follows:

items: Contact the NRS through your preferred NRS call channel, detailed in NRS call numbers and links External Link .
Provide the NRS with the ATO phone number you want to call.

content_text:
## What deferring means
If you have a loan with the ATO (which includes a compulsory repayment or overseas levy) and you can't pay on time, you may apply to defer or amend your compulsory repayment or overseas levy.
This means we will either:
- not raise a repayment (defer)
- reduce the amount of repayment required toward your loan (amend).
## When to apply to defer
You may apply to defer or amend your compulsory repayment or overseas levy if:
- making your repayment would cause you serious hardship
- there are other special reasons, for example natural disasters affecting you death or serious illness other serious or difficult circumstances.
If you apply to defer or amend your repayment under serious hardship, we will ask you for a detailed statement of your household income and expenditure to justify your claim.
We may also ask you to provide further details of income and expenditure and evidence, such as a copy of your most recent payslip.
If you have special circumstances and need to apply to defer or amend your repayment, complete a Defer or amend your compulsory repayment or overseas levy form (NAT 2471).
For more information see Tax support for individuals .
## How to apply for deferral
To apply for a deferral see Defer or amend your compulsory repayment or overseas levy .
For more information see Contact us – Individuals enquiries
## Support for people experiencing vulnerability
If you're facing challenging personal circumstances and need extra help managing your tax obligations, visit Tax support when you need it most for other support options available to people experiencing vulnerability.
## How to use this document
This information is written in a way that is easy to read.
We use pictures to explain some ideas.
We have also provided a PDF of this document so you can read this information offline – If you can't lodge or pay on time - Easy Read (PDF, 3.6MB) This link will download a file
 | This document has been written by the Australian Tax Office or ATO.
 | When you see the words 'we', 'our' or 'us' it means the ATO.
 | This Easy Read document is a summary of another document.
 | You can find the longer document on our website at www.ato.gov.au/Cant-Lodge-Or-Pay
## Lodging and paying tax
 | When you earn money you may need to tell us about it .
 | This means you need to: lodge a tax return pay your tax bills by the due date pay your study or training loan.
 | If you do not pay your tax bill by the due date it becomes a tax debt .
 | A tax debt is when you owe us money. For example, if you did not pay enough tax during the year you will owe us money.
 | A tax return is a form you fill in each year and give to us. In the form you tell us how much money you made and how much tax you have already paid.
## If you can not lodge or pay
 | Talk to us as soon as possible if you can not: lodge a tax return pay your tax debt pay your study or training loan.
 | To tell us about your situation, phone us on 13 11 42 during operating hours .
 | We will need to ask you some questions to prove who you are . This is to protect your information.
This document tells you how to:
 | get help to lodge your tax
 | set up a payment plan to pay your tax
 | ask us to change your tax due date
 | ask us to change your tax amount.
## Help to lodge
 | You must lodge your tax return by 31 October.
 | If you want to understand more about lodging tax we have 2 easier to read documents to help you: Get ready to do your tax How to do your tax
There are many ways to get help with lodging your tax return. You can use:
 | a tax agent
 | Tax Help
 | the National Tax Clinic program.
 | If you can't use these services, you can phone us on 13 11 42 to talk to us about your situation.
 | We will need to ask you some questions to prove who you are . This is to protect your information.
### 1. Tax agent
 | A tax agent is a person who you pay to do your tax.
 | Only registered tax agents can ask for money to do your tax.
Registered means they:
 | have studied to become a tax agent
 | are on our list of tax agents.
 | You can search for a registered tax agent at www.tpb.gov.au External Link
### 2. Tax Help
 | Tax Help is a group of people who help other people do their tax.
 | They do not ask for any money to do this.
 | We have trained these people to know about tax.
 | Not everyone can use this service.
 | Find out more about the Tax Help program .
### 3. National Tax Clinic program
 | The National Tax Clinic program can help people who: can not pay a tax agent do not meet the rules to use Tax Help are having money or personal problems.
 | This program is: paid for by the government run by universities and TAFEs.
 | Students who are learning about tax can help you for free.
 | Trained managers make sure the students give you the right help.
 | The students can help you: in person over the phone through a meeting online.
 | You can also use this program if you are a small business.
 | You need to meet the rules to use this program.
 | Find out more about the National Tax Clinic program External Link .
## Help to pay
We can help you if you can not:
 | pay all your tax
 | pay on time.
 | If you have a tax debt you may be able to set up a payment plan . This is a way to pay using smaller payments over time.
 | If you have a study or training loan you can ask us to defer or amend your payment . Defer means we can change the date of when you need to pay. Amend means we can change the amount you need to pay.
### Payment plan
#### Work out if a payment plan will help you
To work out if a payment plan is a good idea for you, you need to know:
 | your tax debt
 | how much you need to pay straight away
 | how much you can afford to pay each week, fortnight or month. Fortnight means 2 weeks.
 | You can use our payment plan estimator. The payment plan estimator is an online tool. It can help you work out your payment plan.
#### Use the payment plan estimator
 | Open the payment plan estimator .
 | Put in how much your tax debt is. Only put numbers in. Do not put spaces or other symbols in.
Pick when you will pay:
 | one payment a week one payment a fortnight one payment a month.
 | a lump sum payment. Lump sum means you pay your whole tax debt at once.
Choose a date. Select 'Show Result' to get your answer.
 | If you select weekly, fortnightly or monthly payments you will need to pay an upfront amount. Upfront means the amount you must pay within 7 days.
 | You can change the upfront amount to be more or less money.
Then choose how to work out your payment plan. There are 2 ways you can do this.
 | You can choose Repayment amounts. You will need to enter how much you will pay each time.
 | Or you can choose Repayment periods. You will need to enter the start and end dates for your payments.
Select 'Show Result' to get your answer.
 | The tool will show you how much you must pay: in total upfront for each period in interest.
 | You can print the results page to keep for your records.
#### Set up a payment plan
After you use the payment plan estimator you can set up your payment plan with us.
 | Read the important rules about payment plans first.
To set up a payment plan you can:
 | Use ATO online services.
 | Use our automated phone service on 13 28 65. Automated means you will not speak to a person. You will select numbers and symbols using your phone.
 | Phone us on 13 11 42 and speak to a person. We will need to ask you some questions to prove who you are . This is to protect your information.
##### Use ATO online services
 | You will need a myGov account linked to the ATO .
 | When you are logged into ATO online services, from the menu choose: Tax then choose Payment then choose Payment plans.
You can:
 | set up your payment plan
 | choose to be sent SMS or email reminders about payments
 | view a summary of your payment plan
 | change or cancel your payment plan.
##### Use the automated phone service
 | Call the phone service on 13 28 65 .
 | You can ask someone you trust to be with you to help you.
Before you call you will need to know your:
 | tax file number ( TFN ) or Australian business number ( ABN )
 | total amount of your debt.
 | Your TFN is a 9-digit number that identifies you in the tax and system.
 | An ABN is an 11-digit number that a business uses to identify themselves when dealing with other business or with government.
 | The phone service will tell you how to set up the payment plan. It is best to phone in business hours (8:00 am to 6:00 pm on weekdays Monday to Friday).
 | We will connect you to our call centre if you can not use the automated phone service.
### Defer or amend your payment
 | You may have a compulsory repayment for your study or training loan.
 | Compulsory repayment means a payment you must make.
 | You can ask us to defer or amend your compulsory repayment. Defer means you will not have to make a compulsory repayment for the next year.
 | Amend means your payment for the year will be smaller.
 | We can defer or amend if paying will be a serious hardship . Serious hardship is when it is hard to pay for basic living costs like food, housing, transport and medical treatment.
You can also ask us to defer or amend your compulsory repayment if you have been affected by:
 | a natural disaster (like a flood, bushfire or cyclone)
 | serious illness (like cancer)
 | the death of a family member or close friend.
 | You will need to provide us with evidence about your situation .
#### Compulsory repayments you can ask us to defer or amend
You can ask to defer or amend these types of compulsory repayments:
 | Higher Education Loan Program (HELP) Higher Education Contribution Scheme (HECS) VET Student Loan (VSL) Student Financial Supplement Scheme (SFSS) Student Start-up Loan (SSL) ABSTUDY Student Start-up Loan (ABSTUDY SSL) Australian Apprenticeship Support Loan (AASL) – previously known as Trade Support Loan (TSL) HELP, VSL or AASL overseas levy.
#### How to apply
 | You need to fill out a form. You can find it at Defer or amend a repayment .
 | The form has information to help you answer the questions.
 | You can ask someone you trust to help you fill out the form.
 | If you can not fill out the form you can phone us on 1300 650 225 and we will help you.
 | We will need to ask you some questions to prove who you are . This is to protect your information.
## Need help?
 | If you need help you can phone us on 13 11 42 .
 | We will need to ask you some questions to prove who you are . This is to protect your information.
 | If you have difficulty hearing or speaking to people who use a phone, you can contact us through the National Relay Service (NRS).
 | You need to: Call the National Relay Service on 13 36 77 ask the Relay Officer to phone us on 13 11 42 .
 | If you want to speak in a language other than English, phone the Translating and Interpreting Service (TIS National) on 13 14 50 . Tell them: you want to speak to us the language you want to speak in.

## Educational support
We offer free educational support to Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities and culturally and linguistically diverse communities. We deliver informative, tailored presentations on tax and superannuation in Australia to help people understand their responsibilities, obligations and rights. We can also attend large community events that promote government services, if certain criteria are met.
If you want to know more about the support and education we provide, you can email us at CommunityEducation@ato.gov.au to request a presentation for community groups. All presentations are run virtually.
### Watch and listen
- atoTV – Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander languages External Link
- atoTV – multicultural languages External Link
- Audio guides – multicultural languages
- Tax and super experiences – multicultural voices
### Read
- Glossary of common tax and superannuation terms
- Tax time resources in languages other than English
- Forms, publications and factsheets for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples External Link
- Forms, publications and factsheets for multicultural communities External Link (search for your language)
## Contact us
### Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples
Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples can phone our Indigenous Helpline on 13 10 30 (8:00 am to 6:00 pm, Monday to Friday, except public holidays).
Our Indigenous Helpline staff specialise in supporting Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander peoples with things like getting a tax file number, lodging a tax return, or finding your super.
### Translating and interpreting service
If you would like to speak to us in a language other than English, you can use the Translating and Interpreting Service (TIS National).
This free service provides an interpreter in one of these available languages External Link . The service will connect you and an interpreter to us so we can help with your query.
To contact TIS National, between 8:00 am and 6:00 pm AEST, Monday to Friday, phone:
- 13 14 50 if you're in Australia
- +61 3 9268 8332 if you're outside Australia.
TIS National does not provide interpreting services for Indigenous languages. Information about Indigenous interpreters External Link is available on the National Indigenous Australians Agency website.
### Call services for hearing or speech/communication difficulties
If you are d/Deaf, hard of hearing or have a speech/communication difficulty you can contact us through the National Relay Service External Link (NRS), as follows:
- Contact the NRS through your preferred NRS call channel, detailed in NRS call numbers and links External Link .
- Provide the NRS with the ATO phone number you want to call.
